decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to utilize powerful artifacts and synergies to create a fast and aggressive playstyle. By using cards like Goblin Welder and Goblin Engineer to recur key artifacts like Lotus Petal and Mox Opal, the deck can quickly ramp up and overwhelm opponents. Emry, Lurker of the Loch helps to provide card advantage and recursion, while cards like Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Loki, God of Mischief offer additional ways to generate value and disrupt opponents. The goal is to assemble a strong board presence and close out games quickly with aggressive plays.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing cards like Force of Will, Stifle, and Brainstorm to disrupt your opponent's plays. Phyrexian Dreadnought is a key card that can quickly put pressure on your opponent, while cards like Swords to Plowshares and Brazen Borrower can deal with threats efficiently. The deck also includes mana denial with Wasteland and Karakas, as well as card advantage with Nulldrifter and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. Overall, the deck aims to maintain control of the game while setting up for a powerful win condition.

The deck focuses on quickly cheating powerful creatures into play from the graveyard, using a combination of reanimation spells like Reanimate and Animate Dead, as well as disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to protect the combo. A key strategy is to use cards like Show and Tell to put Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play and take control of the game. The deck also includes tools to deal with opposing threats, such as Massacre and Fatal Push, while maintaining card advantage through Brainstorm and Careful Study.

The deck focuses on utilizing lands as resources to control the game, with cards like Crop Rotation and Life from the Loam to access key lands like Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage. With powerful lands like The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale and Maze of Ith, along with disruption from cards like Ghost Quarter and Grafdigger's Cage, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up powerful win conditions like Marit Lage token or Urza's Saga. The deck aims to grind out the opponent with relentless land-based control and threats.

This deck focuses on quickly generating mana and storm count to cast powerful spells and win the game. It utilizes cards like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Ruby Medallion to reduce costs, while cards like Seething Song and Rite of Flame provide additional mana. Burning Wish allows for flexible access to a sideboard toolbox, while Bonus Round and Jeska's Will help to increase the storm count. The goal is to chain together spells, generate a high storm count, and then win the game with a powerful finisher like Storm of Memories or Will of the Jeskai.

The main focus of this deck is to establish a lock on the game by utilizing powerful artifacts to control the board and disrupt your opponent's plans. It aims to slow down the game with cards like Trinisphere and Karn, the Great Creator, while also using cards like Candelabra of Tawnos and Grim Monolith to ramp up mana production. The deck also has a strong late-game win condition with Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms. Overall, the deck is designed to establish control through a combination of prison elements and artifact synergy.

The deck focuses on controlling the board through counterspells and removal spells, while also utilizing card draw and card selection to maintain card advantage. It also includes versatile planeswalkers like Teferi, Time Raveler and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to provide additional control options and card advantage. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and stall the game until it can establish a strong board presence with cards like Monastery Mentor. It also includes utility lands like Karakas and Mystic Sanctuary to further enhance its control capabilities.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize artifacts, particularly blue ones, to create a fast-paced aggressive strategy. By using cards such as Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Mox Opal, as well as powerful artifact creatures like Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of efficient mana ramp and aggressive threats. With a focus on artifact synergy and tempo plays, this deck looks to control the board and apply pressure quickly to secure victory in Legacy 1 vs 1 matches.

The main focus of this deck is to control the board through the use of various creatures and spells, while also utilizing the powerful mana acceleration provided by Gaea's Cradle. By ramping up quickly with cards like Birds of Paradise and Ignoble Hierarch, the deck aims to quickly get to its win conditions like Craterhoof Behemoth and Wight of the Reliquary. The inclusion of cards like Natural Order and Green Sun's Zenith allows for flexibility in searching for key pieces of the deck, while also disrupting opponents with cards like Collector Ouphe and Endurance.

The deck focuses on utilizing graveyard recursion and sacrifice effects to quickly get Hogaak, Arisen Necropolis onto the battlefield. By milling cards with Stitcher's Supplier and utilizing cards like Wight of the Reliquary and Elvish Reclaimer to fuel the graveyard, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with an early Hogaak and other threatening creatures. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the opponent's game plan. Additionally, the deck runs a variety of lands to support its game plan and disrupt opposing strategies.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize Mystic Forge in combination with artifacts like Basalt Monolith, Grim Monolith, and Lotus Petal to generate mana and cast spells for free. Cards like Manifold Key and Voltaic Key can untap key pieces like Basalt Monolith or Mystic Forge to maintain momentum. The deck also includes powerful planeswalkers like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, Eye of the Storms to control the board and win the game. The goal is to assemble a powerful board state and overwhelm the opponent with a combination of combo pieces and powerful threats.

The key concept of this deck is to use Helm of Obedience in combination with Leyline of the Void to exile opponents' entire library, while utilizing cards like Dark Ritual and Chrome Mox to accelerate mana production. The deck also aims to disrupt opponents with hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize and control the board with Sheoldred's Edict. Additionally, cards like Boggart Trawler and Dauthi Voidwalker provide graveyard recursion and protection, while Karn, the Great Creator can shut down opponents' artifacts. The deck is built around a powerful mono black combo strategy focused on graveyard and library manipulation.

The main objective of this deck is to quickly assemble the necessary mana sources and combo pieces to generate a large storm count and cast a lethal Tendrils of Agony or other win condition in a single turn. The deck utilizes powerful mana acceleration cards like Dark Ritual and Lion's Eye Diamond along with tutors like Burning Wish and Infernal Tutor to find key cards like Ad Nauseam and Tendrils of Agony. The deck aims to disrupt opponent's game plan with cards like Duress and Thoughtseize while maintaining a high level of consistency in executing its combo.

The primary focus of this deck is to establish a lock on the game through a combination of artifacts and powerful control spells. By utilizing cards like Trinisphere, Karn, the Great Creator, and Urza's Saga, the deck aims to slow down the opponent's progress while building up its own resources. The inclusion of cards like Cityscape Leveler and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn provide a win condition that is difficult for opponents to answer once the lock is in place. The deck's synergy between artifacts and powerful spells creates a formidable prison that is tough for opponents to break through.

The main concept of this UWx Control Legacy deck is to maintain board control through various counterspells like Force of Will and Force of Negation, removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Prismatic Ending, and card draw with Brainstorm and Ponder. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while gradually building up its own resources to win the game. Teferi, Time Raveler provides additional control and disruption, while cards like Erode and Wrath of the Skies help deal with problematic permanents on the battlefield. Mystic Sanctuary allows for valuable spell recursion, and the mana base supports the deck's color requirements.
